2605.20052.title: PromptRad: Knowledge-Enhanced Multi-Label Prompt-Tuning for Low-Resource Radiology Report Labeling
2605.20052.paper_summary: Automatic report labeling facilitates the identification of clinical findings from unstructured text and enables large-scale annotation for medical imaging research. Existing rule-based labelers struggle with the diverse descriptions in clinical reports, while fine-tuning pre-trained language models (PLMs) requires large amounts of labeled data that are often unavailable in clinical settings. In this paper, we propose PromptRad, a knowledge-enhanced multi-label \textbf{prompt}-tuning approach for \textbf{rad}iology report labeling under low-resource settings. PromptRad reformulates multi-label classification as masked language modeling and incorporates synonyms from the UMLS Metathesaurus into a multi-word verbalizer to enrich category representations. By fine-tuning the PLM without additional classification layers, PromptRad requires substantially less labeled data than conventional fine-tuning. Experiments on liver CT reports show that PromptRad outperforms dictionary-based and fine-tuning baselines with only 32 labeled training examples, and achieves competitive performance with GPT-4 despite using a much smaller model. Further analysis demonstrates that PromptRad captures complex negation patterns more effectively than existing methods, making it a promising solution for report labeling in data-scarce clinical scenarios. Our code is available at https://github.com/ila-lab/PromptRad.
2605.20052.paper_summary_zh: 自動報告標記有助於從非結構化文本中識別臨床發現，並使醫學影像研究能夠進行大規模標註。現有的基於規則的標記器在臨床報告中面對多樣的描述時表現不佳，而微調預訓練語言模型（PLMs）則需要大量標記數據，這在臨床環境中通常無法獲得。在本文中，我們提出了PromptRad，一種在低資源環境下進行放射學報告標記的知識增強多標籤\textbf{提示}調整方法。PromptRad將多標籤分類重新表述為掩碼語言建模，並將UMLS Metathesaurus中的同義詞納入多詞動詞化器，以豐富類別表示。通過在不添加額外分類層的情況下微調PLM，PromptRad所需的標記數據顯著少於傳統的微調方法。對肝臟CT報告的實驗表明，PromptRad在僅使用32個標記訓練示例的情況下超越了基於字典和微調的基準，並在使用更小模型的情況下仍能與GPT-4達到競爭性能。進一步的分析表明，PromptRad比現有方法更有效地捕捉複雜的否定模式，使其成為數據稀缺的臨床場景中報告標記的有前景解決方案。我們的代碼可在https://github.com/ila-lab/PromptRad獲得。
2605.20052.author: Ying-Jia Lin et.al.
2605.20052.authors: Ying-Jia Lin, Tzu-Chin Lo, Ping-Chien Li, Chi-Tung Cheng, Chien-Hung Liao, Hung-Yu Kao
